commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=0af64e07d48876e731984729490d400c375c0b66)
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates strict-agentic planning-only retry detection and tests to treat pure `sleep` bash/exec metadata as no-progress while preserving real command progress.

PR surface: Source +36, Tests +79. Total +115 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible. Current main rejects non-`update_plan` tool metadata and `toolUse` terminal turns before planning-only text retry, and the PR head still misses the current `host=node` production metadata suffix.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Queue a narrow repair because the remaining blocker is a mechanical classifier/test mismatch against the current exec metadata contract.

target.title: fix(agents): detect dummy sleep planning-only turns
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the strict-agentic planning-only guard treated a `toolUse` terminal turn and any non-`update_plan` tool meta as concrete progress, so a dummy `bash` sleep could bypass the retry.
- Solution: classify `update_plan` plus pure `sleep` `bash`/`exec` production metadata as no-progress tool activity, including workdir/node/pty/elevated display suffixes, while keeping shell groups and ordinary commands as progress.
- What changed: `resolvePlanningOnlyRetryInstruction()` now distinguishes no-progress sleep metas from real command activity using the same bash metadata shape produced by `inferToolMetaFromArgs()`.
- What did NOT change: ordinary command execution, file mutations, messaging deliveries, async started tools, shell groups, and multi-tool progress still suppress planning-only retries.

Fixes #64883.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: A strict-agentic assistant turn that only says it will act and calls `bash` with production metadata for `sleep 0.1` should still be treated as planning-only, including production workdir/node suffixes; production metadata for `sleep 0.1; (touch /tmp/openclaw-sentinel)` and ordinary `bash` commands should not be replayed as planning-only.

Real environment tested: Local Linux checkout with patched production `inferToolMetaFromArgs()` and `resolvePlanningOnlyRetryInstruction()` loaded through `tsx`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 exec tsx -e 'import { inferToolMetaFromArgs } from "./src/agents/embedded-agent-utils.ts"; import { resolvePlanningOnlyRetryInstruction } from "./src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts"; const base = { provider: "openai", modelId: "gpt-5.4", executionContract: "strict-agentic", prompt: "Please inspect the code, make the change, and run the checks.", aborted: false, timedOut: false }; const productionMeta = (args: Record<string, unknown>) => inferToolMetaFromArgs("bash", args, { detailMode: "explain" }); const attempt = (meta: string | undefined) => ({ assistantTexts: ["I\x27ll update the code now."], clientToolCalls: undefined, yieldDetected: false, didSendDeterministicApprovalPrompt: false, didSendViaMessagingTool: false, messagingToolSentTexts: [], messagingToolSentMediaUrls: [], messagingToolSentTargets: [], lastToolError: undefined, lastAssistant: { stopReason: "toolUse" }, itemLifecycle: { startedCount: 1, completedCount: 1, activeCount: 0 }, replayMetadata: { hadPotentialSideEffects: true, replaySafe: false }, toolMetas: [{ toolName: "bash", meta }] }); const sleepMeta = productionMeta({ command: "sleep 0.1" }); const scopedSleepMeta = productionMeta({ command: "sleep 0.1", workdir: "/tmp", host: "node", node: "builder-1" }); const shellGroupMeta = productionMeta({ command: "sleep 0.1; (touch /tmp/openclaw-sentinel)", workdir: "/tmp", host: "node", node: "builder-1" }); const ordinaryMeta = productionMeta({ command: "git status --short", workdir: "/tmp", host: "node", node: "builder-1" }); console.log("production sleep meta:", sleepMeta); console.log("production scoped sleep meta:", scopedSleepMeta); console.log("production shell group meta:", shellGroupMeta); console.log("production ordinary bash meta:", ordinaryMeta); console.log("dummy sleep retry:", resolvePlanningOnlyRetryInstruction({ ...base, attempt: attempt(sleepMeta) })?.includes("Act now") ?? false); console.log("scoped sleep retry:", resolvePlanningOnlyRetryInstruction({ ...base, attempt: attempt(scopedSleepMeta) })?.includes("Act now") ?? false); console.log("sleep shell group retry:", resolvePlanningOnlyRetryInstruction({ ...base, attempt: attempt(shellGroupMeta) }) ?? "null"); console.log("ordinary bash retry:", resolvePlanningOnlyRetryInstruction({ ...base, attempt: attempt(ordinaryMeta) }) ?? "null");'
```

Evidence after fix: Console output from the patched production metadata and retry helpers:
```text
production sleep meta: sleep 0.1
production scoped sleep meta: sleep 0.1 (in /tmp), node: builder-1
production shell group meta: sleep 0.1; (touch /tmp/openclaw-sentinel) (in /tmp), node: builder-1
production ordinary bash meta: check git status (in /tmp), node: builder-1
dummy sleep retry: true
scoped sleep retry: true
sleep shell group retry: null
ordinary bash retry: null
```

Observed result after fix: The same strict-agentic plan-only prose plus production-generated `sleep 0.1` bash meta now returns the act-now retry instruction for both base and workdir/node display suffixes, while production-generated shell-group and ordinary bash metas return `null` and are not replayed as planning-only.

What was not tested: No full embedded runner retry-exhaustion integration was added; this change is covered at the production metadata and retry-decision seam used by the runner.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Focused helper regression tests using production bash metadata generation.
- Target test file: `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts`.
- Scenario locked in: strict-agentic `toolUse` turn with plan-only prose and production `inferToolMetaFromArgs()` bash meta `sleep 0.1` must return the planning-only retry instruction.
- Boundary guardrail: ordinary production non-sleep bash meta, production shell-group bash meta, production workdir/node sleep suffixes, and production ` · pty` sleep suffix handling stay covered so real command activity is still treated as progress/side effect.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: `inferToolMetaFromArgs()` produces the bash metadata stored by the subscription path, and `resolvePlanningOnlyRetryInstruction()` is the production decision point that the runner calls before retrying or surfacing the strict-agentic blocked state.

## Verification

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 tsgo:core
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 tsgo:core:test
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 exec oxlint --deny=warn --ignore-path=.oxlintignore src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Verification output captured locally:

```text
Focused Vitest:
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  113 passed (113)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 22.68s

tsgo:core passed
tsgo:core:test passed
oxlint touched files passed
git diff --check passed
```

## Root Cause

- Root cause: planning-only detection counted every non-`update_plan` tool meta as progress and rejected any terminal stop reason other than `stop`, so a dummy sleep tool call with `stopReason: "toolUse"` skipped the text-based planning-only detector.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no no-progress tool classification for dummy delay commands before applying tool progress, replay-safety, item lifecycle, and `toolUse` terminal checks; the sleep classifier must stay limited to production sleep metadata so shell grouping remains protected as progress.
